description: The article from ilrestodelcarlino.it reports on an investigation led by Professor Fabio Giglietto at the University of Urbino Carlo Bo, focusing on how algorithms influence the world, including disinformation and manipulation tactics.

title: L'articolo 21 nella gabbia di Facebook
snippet: The article references recent research by Fabio Giglietto, professor of Sociology of Cultural and Communicative Processes at the University of Urbino, which investigates the effect of Meta's policy to reduce political content on Facebook from 2021 onwards.
description: The article references recent research by Professor Fabio Giglietto from the University of Urbino, which investigates the effect of Meta's policy to reduce political content on Facebook.

title: teleambiente.tv
description: The article from April 18, 2024, reports on a conference at the University of Urbino titled "Meta, algorithms and extremists: a conference discusses their relationship with politics," featuring Fabio Giglietto, Professor of Digital Sociology, as a speaker.

title: Secondo uno studio dell'università di Urbino l'algoritmo di Facebook ora punisce la politica ufficiale e premia gli estremisti antieuropei. Pd e FdI verso una legge bipartisan sulle piattaforme - Open

Full list of Panels 2026 - International Conference on Social Media & Society
snippet: The page lists Fabio Giglietto as a participant in the panel "Meta Content Library Community Roundtable – Where to for Social Media Research?" at the 2026 International Conference on Social Media & Society.
description: Fabio Giglietto is listed as a participant in a panel on social media research at the upcoming 2026 International Conference on Social Media & Society.

title: Communication and Inequalities in Context (ICA 2026 Conference Program)
snippet: Fabio Giglietto is listed as an author of the paper "Clicking Politics on an Algorithmic Feed: Political Efficacy and Longitudinal Information Selection," which is scheduled to be presented at the International Communication Association (ICA) 2026 conference in June 2026.
description: A draft program for the ICA 2026 conference lists Fabio Giglietto as an author presenting research on political efficacy and information selection on algorithmic feeds.
